description: Curated past maintainer rejections (and one accepted counter-exhibit) used as adversarial pre-ship review exhibits. Verbatim from doldecomp/melee PRs #2655-#2659 reviewed by PsiLupan. Phase 4's banned_patterns store will feed retrieval; until then this static set is loaded as-is.

exhibits.comment: This is a regression that we've talked about before. Using an extern to make a function match is just due to data ordering.
exhibits.pattern: added `extern const f32 lbl_804DA60C;` to dodge data ordering

exhibits.comment: This is a regression and should be a call to something like `HSD_JObjGetTranslation2` since it's only has a single assert here.
exhibits.pattern: open-coded `__assert("jobj.h", 979, "jobj")` instead of the inline helper/HSD_ASSERT
HSD_ASSERT replaced with raw __assert + extern'd filename string

exhibits.comment: Don't change this to the extern char. It was already declared and it should be removed in the other place using it.
exhibits.pattern: string literal assert arg replaced with extern char symbol grKg_803E1A00

exhibits.comment: As mentioned on other PRs, this is a regression. It's trying to fix sdata2 ordering.
exhibits.pattern: added `extern const f32 grKg_804DAFA0/grKg_804DAFA4` self-TU sdata2 externs

exhibits.comment: This is a regression. These strings should not be access this way.
exhibits.pattern: hand-packed `static char lbl_803EFB60[0xA8]` string blob + #define pointer-offset accessors

exhibits.comment: Similar regression to the previous. Matching because of externs is not correct. It means data ordering isn't finished.
exhibits.pattern: OSReport string literals replaced with `extern char un_803FF074[0xA8]` + offset arithmetic

exhibits.comment: You submitted this change before and it was called out as a regression. Please make sure you're reviewing these PRs a bit more closely before submitting, as I'd prefer not to be calling out the same issues again and again.
exhibits.pattern: resubmission of previously-rejected packed string blob `static char lbl_8040A540[0x268]`

exhibits.comment: I don't like declaration order being done like this, but removing it is a regression.
exhibits.pattern: ACCEPTED (counter-exhibit): forward externs whose definitions exist later in the SAME file in binary order — style note only, not a reject
